title: Golden Transcripts, net.Pipe, Differential Tests, Fuzzing, Race, and Benchmarks · Stakeholder Go Mission
summary: A proxy, origin, and security response group. They need shared parser-differential corpora, smuggling and splitting defense, bounded slow-client behavior, explicit state invariants, golden transcripts, net.Pipe schedules, differential tests, fuzzing, race, leak, and capacity gates. Planned artifact: Deliver Adversarial Multi-Hop Protocol Verification Lab with acceptance evidence, rubric defense, reflection, and a changed-constraint extension. Learners progress through prediction, explicit theor…
objectives: Test HTTP/2 or HTTP/3 to HTTP/1.1 translation, proxy chains, cache parsing, and backend normalization with the same adversarial corpus.
Prove connection, goroutine, timer, buffer, queue, tunnel, temporary data, and metric-cardinality cleanup for every terminal state.
Define invariants for cursor bounds, monotonic consumption, one framing choice, one response order, one close owner, and no effects before validation.
Preserve previously learned constraints and attach observable changed-case evidence.

steps.title: Commit to the mission model · Project Downgrade Consistency Gate
steps.instruction:
project case — A project protocol team handles case 9033 in a canary edge service advertising HTTP/3 with bounded fallback. Build deterministic Go evidence to gate parser work on transcripts, fragmentation, differential, fuzz, and leak evidence; preserve this operating constraint: diagnostics must omit credentials and attacker-controlled metric labels; then split every structural delimiter across a different read and retain the same result. Browser code models bounded byte and state decisions only. Sockets, listeners, live TLS, proxies, packet capture, fuzz campaigns, races, load, HTTP/2, HTTP/3, and production behavior require explicit authorized transfer gates. Competency probe: Test HTTP/2 or HTTP/3 to HTTP/1.1 translation, proxy chains, cache parsing, and backend normalization with the same adversarial corpus.

What must be established before implementation?

Evidence target: Commit to the mission model · Project Downgrade Consistency Gate.
steps.why: The decision supplies observable evidence for test http/2 or http/3 to http/1.1 translation, proxy chains, cache parsing, and backend normalization with the same adversarial corpus. For Commit to the mission model · Project Downgrade Consistency Gate, keep Downgrade Consistency Gate connected to executable Go behavior evidence.

steps.instruction:
project case — A project protocol team handles case 9041 in a privacy-sensitive proxy translating between two parser implementations. Build deterministic Go evidence to gate parser work on transcripts, fragmentation, differential, fuzz, and leak evidence; preserve this operating constraint: no malformed framing may be normalized and forwarded to another recipient; then lower one byte or time budget and prove rejection occurs before allocation or dispatch. Browser code models bounded byte and state decisions only. Sockets, listeners, live TLS, proxies, packet capture, fuzz campaigns, races, load, HTTP/2, HTTP/3, and production behavior require explicit authorized transfer gates. Competency probe: Run race, leak, allocation, benchmark, profiling, and changed-load gates while separating correctness from capacity claims.

One precondition fails while output still looks plausible. What is the correct response?

options.text: Keep the plausible output and hide the assumption to avoid confusing the stakeholder. That claim does not survive the test an acceptance boundary evidence boundary.
For test an acceptance boundary, run one convenient Race Leak Benchmark Gate happy path, omit ownership, error, changed input, goroutine stop, and full-toolchain checks, then accept plausible output.
Reject the unsupported conclusion, name the violated Race Leak Benchmark Gate condition, repair the responsible layer, and rerun the boundary trace.

steps.title: Defend the stakeholder delivery · Project Race Leak Benchmark Gate
steps.instruction:
Write the project evidence note for Race Leak Benchmark Gate: explain the governing condition, one failure, and how this concrete case was verified — A project protocol team handles case 9042 in a regional status service with slow assistive and low-bandwidth clients. Build deterministic Go evidence to gate parser work on transcripts, fragmentation, differential, fuzz, and leak evidence; preserve this operating constraint: no malformed framing may be normalized and forwarded to another recipient; then append a second pipelined message and prove the first parser leaves the exact residual bytes. Browser code models bounded byte and state decisions only. Sockets, listeners, live TLS, proxies, packet capture, fuzz campaigns, races, load, HTTP/2, HTTP/3, and production behavior require explicit authorized transfer gates. Competency probe: Run race, leak, allocation, benchmark, profiling, and changed-load gates while separating correctness from capacity claims.

content.title: Evidence defense
content.text: Use the case, one assumption, one observable result, and one limitation. Do not copy the competency statement: Run race, leak, allocation, benchmark, profiling, and changed-load gates while separating correctness from capacity claims.
